channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: New Pokémon Pokopia Update Fixes Many Broken Quests—Full Patch Notes
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Pokopia might be the best Pokémon game in years, maybe ever, but it’s not without its flaws. One of the most notable downsides is certain Requests not behaving as intended, leaving players unable to progress—a new Pokémon Pokopia looks to fix this.

Having some Request trouble in Pokémon Pokopia? Ditto. Certain Requests have been as troublesome as high-level Pokémon when you don’t have enough Gym badges—they won’t listen.

Pokémon Pokopia Fixes Several Key Issues

Nintendo is looking to fix various Pokémon Pokopia Requests, such as Withered Wasteland, Bleak Beach, and Rocky Ridges, to overcome unlock conditions and progression issues.

If any of these quests have been giving you grief, help is on the way. Below are the confirmed patch notes and what the original problem was.

All Pokémon Pokopia March 17 Patch Notes

Confirmed Issues

  • During the Withered Wasteland request “Help make a home!”, Squirtle moves to the top of a tree and cannot be spoken to, causing it to become impossible to proceed with the request.
  • During the Bleak Beach request “Find the Pokémon Center!”, if you break the cracked block in the bridge before Professor Tangrowth crosses it, it becomes difficult to proceed with the request.
  • During the Bleak Beach request “Find the Pokémon Center!”, if you perform actions in a certain order, Professor Tangrowth’s bridge repair event will not trigger, causing it to become impossible to proceed with the request.
  • If you perform actions in a certain order at Rocky Ridges, the event in which you meet Rotom will not trigger.
  • If the Rocky Ridges request “Clear the path!” is triggered under certain conditions, it becomes difficult to proceed with the request.
  • Spinarak’s type is listed incorrectly in the Pokédex.

Plans for Improvement

We plan to implement improvements regarding the following:

  • During the Withered Wasteland request “Rock Smash your way to treasure!”, if you place another block at the same location as the cracked block close to Hitmonchan, it will make it hard to see how you should proceed.
  • During the Bleak Beach request “To Snorlax”, if you place another block at the same location as the cracked block close to Snorlax, it will make it hard to see how you should proceed.

Plans for Future Support

We plan to distribute the update data with fixes for the above issues in the near future.

If you have encountered one of these issues in your copy of the game, it will be resolved if you download the update data.

We will continue to investigate other issues.

Credit to Nintendo for the patch notes.

Will any of these changes come as a welcome relief for your save file? Are any other Requests being overlooked, or issues in general? Comment your thoughts via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Make sure you read about what happened to the Humans in Pokémon Pokopia, if you haven’t already. We also have more handy guides, including Pokémon Pokopia Codes.

MOBILE APP

item.title: PlayStation Portal Update Adds New ‘High Quality Mode’ & More In March Update
item.content:encoded:
March 2026 brings a brand-new PlayStation Portal update. The most significant improvement is the new 1080p High Quality mode, which will make your gameplay smoother than butter and more glorious than a beautifully manicured garden.

The PlayStation Portal has been a great success for Sony. The ability to play PS5 games on the go is a no-brainer, and Sony continues to support the device. A March 2026 update has added a huge new feature and made other vital tweaks.

1080p High Quality Mode & Several Enhancements In PlayStation Portal Update

A March 17 PlayStation Blog discussed the increasing presence of the PlayStation Portal and how more people than ever are engaging with it: “Cloud Streaming monthly users grew by 162% year-over-year in January, and over 50% of PS Portal users are now PlayStation Plus Premium subscribers.”

But you didn’t come here for that; you came for news on the 1080p High-Quality Mode.

Once the update is installed, you can choose an upgraded 1080p High Quality mode during Remote Play and Cloud Streaming, which ‘prioritizes higher video quality and uses more data.’

This should be a nice improvement over the 1080p Standard mode. Also, Sony has announced improvements to the overall streaming experience: Product pages, game invites, trophy information, and search functions have all been enhanced for greater accessibility and navigation.

Finally, the update now gives new players a ‘smoother onboarding experience’ to get you suited and booted in a flash.

The update goes live on March 18, and you can start enjoying all the new features right away!

Are you enjoying the PlayStation Portal? What else does Sony need to address in future updates? Share your ideas via the Insider Gaming Discord. You never know who’s listening!

Check out the first Crimson Desert PlayStation 5 console gameplay footage and Starfield and ‘Future Bethesda Titles’ using AI-Driven DLSS 5 Technology for more hot gaming news.

MOBILE APP

item.title: First Crimson Desert PlayStation 5 Console Gameplay Footage Officially Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Crimson Desert looks like one of 2026’s most ambitious games, in a year with GTA 6 after all! Pearl Abyss has marketed Crimson Desert well, but the noticeable lack of PlayStation and Xbox console footage has been a huge worry. The wait is finally over.

With the Crimson Desert review embargo looming, everyone has been wondering how the game performs. Does it run great on PC but not on console? The gaming community has been burned by similar situations before, but if you’re wondering how Crimson Desert handles on console, you can stop imagining it and start watching it.

Crimson Desert PS5 Gameplay Finally Shown Off

The official PlayStation Japan YouTube channel has unveiled over 20 minutes of PlayStation 5 console gameplay footage ahead of the game’s much-hyped launch.

Pearl Abyss has previously said that the company is ‘not hiding anything.’

Now, it definitely isn’t. The main X page let players know: “#CrimsonDesert gameplay on the PlayStation 5 base console has premiered on PlayStation Japan’s PLAY! PLAY! PLAY! Along with a new boss..!”

You can check it out for yourself below.

One misconception to clean up is the comments being turned off for the video. This is pretty much a default action on all PlayStation Japan YouTube videos, and not something done specifically for Crimson Desert.

Has everyone been worrying about nothing? How does Crimson Desert look on PS5? Share your thoughts via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Also, check out if Crimson Desert runs on the Steam Deck and how to earn Crimson Desert Twitch Drops.
